arts.ttl: 'Another owner went missing': Former Reading forward on 'frustrating' loan move
arts.sum: Former Reading forward Billy Sharp admitted that 2013/14 was 'frustrating' as a loan extension was not possible. The 39-year-old is an EFL legend, scoring more goals in the second, third and fourth tier than anyone else. This is most prominently at clubs such as Sheffield United and Doncaster Rovers, where he is still leading the line today. Back in 2013/14, the forward was surplus to requirements at Premier League Southampton but had finished as the top scorer for Nottingham Forest in 2012/13. Moving to Berkshire in September 2013, the striker was injured shortly after arriving and had to wait until December to make his debut, scoring in a win over Charlton Athletic.
